content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
RISK-ON environment — Normal volatility 28.9% with broad participation (Breadth 6/8). Market is favoring cyclicals and holdings while defensive tech and retail lag.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Holding & Investment leading: DOHOL (1W: +10.92% | 1M: +2.57%) ABOVE TREND; Construction & REIT showing strong bounce: TKFEN (1W: +16.25% | 1M: -4.73%) RECOVERY; Heavy Industry supportive: KRDMD (1W: +6.43% | 1M: +4.11%) ABOVE TREND.

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Retail & Food lagging: SOKM (1W: -6.24% | 1M: -9.71%) BELOW TREND and MGROS (1W: -4.59% | 1M: -10.07%) BELOW TREND; Technology & Telecom soft: TCELL (1W: -1.59% | 1M: -4.70%) BELOW TREND.

## STOCK PERFORMANCE

4. Top Performing Stocks?
TKFEN (1W: +16.25% | 1M: -4.73%) RECOVERY, DOHOL (1W: +10.92% | 1M: +2.57%) ABOVE TREND, HALKB (1W: +8.60% | 1M: +9.18%) ABOVE TREND, CCOLA (1W: +8.05% | 1M: -5.83%) RECOVERY, KRDMD (1W: +6.43% | 1M: +4.11%) ABOVE TREND.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
PETKM (1W: -7.21% | 1M: -5.13%) BELOW TREND, SOKM (1W: -6.24% | 1M: -9.71%) BELOW TREND, AEFES (1W: -5.23% | 1M: -14.49%) BELOW TREND, MGROS (1W: -4.59% | 1M: -10.07%) BELOW TREND, TTKOM (1W: -3.75% | 1M: -8.54%) BELOW TREND.

6. Momentum Stocks?
TKFEN (1W: +16.25% | 1M: -4.73%) RECOVERY, DOHOL (1W: +10.92% | 1M: +2.57%) ABOVE TREND, HALKB (1W: +8.60% | 1M: +9.18%) ABOVE TREND, CCOLA (1W: +8.05% | 1M: -5.83%) RECOVERY, TUPRS (1W: +5.53% | 1M: +12.83%) ABOVE TREND.

## SECTOR OUTLOOK

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Mixed but constructive — HALKB (1W: +8.60% | 1M: +9.18%) ABOVE TREND and GARAN (1W: +2.54% | 1M: -10.24%) RECOVERY show pockets of strength, while AKBNK (1W: -2.28% | 1M: -9.77%) BELOW TREND, ISCTR (1W: -0.54% | 1M: -13.13%) BELOW TREND and VAKBN (1W: -0.99% | 1M: -3.39%) BELOW TREND remain weak.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Heavy industry is the market’s engine — ASELS (1W: +3.72% | 1M: +14.47%) ABOVE TREND, KRDMD (1W: +6.43% | 1M: +4.11%) ABOVE TREND, FROTO (1W: +3.32% | 1M: -6.11%) RECOVERY and ARCLK (1W: +4.49% | 1M: -8.58%) RECOVERY point to durable cyclical interest.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Selective strength: TUPRS (1W: +5.53% | 1M: +12.83%) ABOVE TREND and TRALT (1W: +4.92% | 1M: +14.60%) ABOVE TREND are leading, while PETKM (1W: -7.21% | 1M: -5.13%) BELOW TREND and SASA (1W: -0.88% | 1M: -10.67%) BELOW TREND highlight dispersion.

## PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Risk-on stance: overweight cyclicals (energy, heavy industry) and select holdings (DOHOL, SAHOL) while trimming structurally weak retail and tech names; keep cash buffer for near-term volatility spikes given normal vol regime.

## NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
TUPRS (1W: +5.53% | 1M: +12.83%) ABOVE TREND, TKFEN (1W: +16.25% | 1M: -4.73%) RECOVERY, DOHOL (1W: +10.92% | 1M: +2.57%) ABOVE TREND, ASELS (1W: +3.72% | 1M: +14.47%) ABOVE TREND, PETKM (1W: -7.21% | 1M: -5.13%) BELOW TREND.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Holding & Investment for allocation flows, Heavy Industry for earnings/momentum, Energy & Chemicals for oil-price linkage and corporate catalysts.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
Watch XU100 momentum around the recent 1-month range — breach of the 1-month high would confirm continuation; failure to hold near-term support would shift tone. Macro/cash triggers: global risk appetite, oil moves, CBRT communications and company-specific earnings/corporate news.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
RISK-ON market with normal volatility (28.9%) and broad sector participation (6/8 positive); cyclicals and holdings are leading while retail and tech lag. Favor overweight in energy and heavy industry names (TUPRS, ASELS, KRDMD) and selectively add high-quant holdings (DOHOL) while trimming weak retail/tech exposures.
